notes.note: The sender is Cicero's brother Quintus; 'Marcus' is the orator himself, jokingly cast as the advocate Tiro will need to defend his failure to write.

notes.anchor: she would set her seal even on empty flasks
notes.note: Quintus recalls their mother Helvia sealing even emptied wine-jars, so a slave could not claim a jar had always been empty rather than secretly drained. The point: write even when there is nothing to report.
